@orediaz.title: ✨ Un momento épico entre Rubi y Jacky 😅 Me hacen mi consulta para un cambio de imagen… y por un momento ¡me puse desesperado pensando en qué hacer! Pero, ¿por qué no? 🤷🏼‍♂️ Si a eso me dedico 💇‍♂️💥 👉🏻 El resultado lo pueden ver en los siguientes videos… 👉🏻 O si quieren, aquí les dejo la imagen completa: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTpVjcwjit9/?igsh=MWp4aHA2ajExcHN5YQ== Todas las maravillas suceden aquí 👇🏻 📍 4343 Telephone Rd, Houston, Texas  #CambioDeImagen #HairTransformation #AntesYDespués #HairJourney #RubiYJacky

@orediaz.name: Orestes Diaz Mejias
@orediaz.title: ✨ Desde Dallas hasta Houston por un cambio de imagen con propósito ✨ La hermosa Katy recorrió kilómetros para confiar en nosotros y vivir su transformación. Tras un análisis profundo, descubrimos que aunque los tonos oscuros y su color natural ya le favorecen, los colores cálidos simplemente la iluminan de una manera espectacular 🤎✨ No se trata de cambiar por cambiar… Se trata de potenciar la belleza que ya existe, de armonizar, de resaltar la esencia y llevar el color correcto en el momento perfecto. Katy es hermosa, sí… pero cuando eliges los tonos adecuados, la belleza se eleva a otro nivel 🔥 Así se vive un verdadero cambio de imagen: con análisis, intención, arte y mucho amor por el detalle 💫 📍 4343 Telephone Rd, Houston, Texas 77087 #Hazards #CambioDeImagen #DesdeDallas #ColoresCálidos #AnálisisDeColor

@therealmustbecindy.name: Mustbecindy
@therealmustbecindy.title: I have been holding this in for months and months 😭🥺 Because I was dying for the world to see the little girl in me who finally found the kind of love she prayed for her whole life. When I was a little girl, I always believed the safest love would be my dad… and then one day, the love of my life. I didn’t know how long I would have to wait for that second part. I just knew my heart longed for it. And it took 32 years. 32 years of growing, breaking, healing, learning, unlearning… 32 years of wondering if a man would ever love me gently, protect me softly, choose me fully, and stay. And then God sent him. The kind of love that feels safe. The kind that feels like home. The kind that heals parts of you you didn’t even realize were still hurting. The little girl in me finally feels chosen. Finally feels protected. Finally feels loved for the rest of her life 🤍😭 And that alone makes every year of waiting worth it

@thehealthyhur.name: Taylor Rose ✨
@thehealthyhur.title: #ad Your hair isn’t just responding to what you put on it… it’s responding to the environment it’s growing from. And that’s exactly why I’ve been using @Raw Sugar Living Scalp Restore shampoo + conditioner lately. As a trichologist, I see this all the time— so much focus on serums and treatments, but not enough attention on the scalp itself. If your scalp feels congested or out of balance, your hair will reflect that. I’ve been simplifying my routine and focusing more on gentle, balanced cleansing, and the Raw Sugar Scalp Restore shampoo + conditioner has been helping me reset my scalp without stripping it. It’s formulated with aloe, niacinamide, and jojoba oil to help support the scalp barrier and keep things feeling calm and hydrated. And as someone who takes a more holistic approach (and loves animals), I really appreciate that it’s vegan and not tested on animals 🤍 You can find it at @target been wanting to try it! #rawsugarpartner #rawsugarliving #scalphealth #trichologist

@jennypenaxo.name: Jenny Peña 🦋
@jennypenaxo.title: Must-try Frijoles Puercos and Steak Tacos ✨ #ad #CholulaPartner @Cholula Hot Sauce #cholula #cremosa #cholularecipes  Ingredients for frijoles puerco:  • 3 lbs of mayocoba beans (frijoles peruanos) • Salt (to taste) • 1 cup of pork lard • 3 corn tortillas (store bought or homemade) • 18 ounces of pork chorizo •  9 cups of bean broth  • 3 pickled jalapeños, finely chopped • 3 chipotle peppers in adobo sauce, finely chopped • 3 tablespoons of Cholula® Original Hot Sauce • Queso fresco Ingredients for beef tacos • 1/2 tbsp Oil • 3.5 lbs of beef (I used 100% grass fed beef) • 2 packets of Cholula® Original Taco - Medium Recipe Mix • Handmade corn tortillas (or store bought) • 3 avocados • Cholula® Original - Medium Salsa Recipe: 1. In a large pot with four quarts of boiling water, add 3 lbs of rinsed mayocoba beans (frijoles peruanos). Reduce heat to low-medium and cover for about 1.5 hours. 2. Cut beef into bite-size pieces. 3. Heat up half tablespoon of oil in a pan. Once it's hot, add your meat in and stir. 4. Season with two packets of Cholula® Original Taco - Medium Recipe Mix. Stir again, reduce heat to low and cover for 20 minutes or until tender. 5. At the 1.5 hour mark, add salt to taste to your pot of beans. Cover again for another hour or until they’re nice and soft. 6. In another pot, on medium- high heat, melt 1 cup of pork lard. 7. Rip 3 tortillas in pieces and fry in lard until golden. Drain tortilla chips on paper towel-lined bowl. Reduce heat to medium. 8. In that same pot I’m going to add my chorizo, slightly covering with a lid to avoid splattering. Break apart and cook for about 4-5 minutes 9. Add your beans in along with the cooking liquid and tortilla chips. Cover and let simmer for about 10 minutes. 10. Uncover and add pickled jalapeños, chipotle peppers and Cholula® Original Hot Sauce. Stir to combine. 11. Mash everything by hand with a potato masher or use a hand blender/blender for a smoother consistency. Turn heat off. 12. Serve to your liking. I topped my steak tacos with onion, cilantro, avocado and Cholula® Original - Medium Salsa and my frijoles puercos tacos with crumbled queso fresco. Enjoy!

@lisa.beautify.name: Lisa Baisl
@lisa.beautify.title: Korean Skincare is NOT a Scam (k-beauty is still my fav), but you can’t rely only on skincare products to achieve great skin.  Our diet plays an important role, so I recommend incorporating skin food recipes into your diet. One of my favorite #skinfood recipes is Kimchi - a traditional, slightly spicy Korean sauerkraut made from fermented vegetables.  One of the advantages of fermentation is that it preserves the goodness of the vegetables. In addition, some bacteria synthesize new nutrients, such as vitamins C and B. I eat Kimchi with many things, from fried rice to dumplings or straight out of the jar.  I still have a lot to learn about kimchi-making from Koreans (there are over a hundred different kinds!), but the recipe for this simple Kimchi is a great place to start.  Ingredients: 1 Chinese cabbage 1 carrot Spice paste: 1, 5 tbsp glutinous rice flour 1 onion 1 garlic glove some ginger 3 spring onions 3 tbsp fish sauce 50 gr. gochugaru (Korean chili powder) . . #skinfood #kimchi #skinfoodrecipes #kbeauty

@kokofaceyoga.name: Koko Face Yoga
@kokofaceyoga.title: If you notice jowls at a younger age, it can be from natural fullness or how you hold your face at rest. Small habits, like using the lower face muscles too much or how you form sounds like A E I O U, can affect your facial muscles over time. Since these habits happen all day, even slight tension can make a difference, so being aware of your resting face and speaking patterns is key. #KokoHayashi #KokoFaceYoga #FaceYoga #FacePosture #CorrectPosture

@kokofaceyoga.name: Koko Face Yoga
@kokofaceyoga.title: If your nostrils flare often, it can gradually make them look wider. This usually comes from habits like “ee” expressions that overactivate the area. By softening those habits and keeping better tongue posture, you reduce unnecessary movement and tension. Small habits shape your features over time. #KokoHayashi #KokoFaceYoga #FaceYoga #FacePosture #CorrectPosture
